name: vmarkd
description: vMarkd — a full-featured visual markdown editor.
author: Sławomir Piochacz

untrustedWorkspaces.description: vMarkd renders and edits markdown, but defers writing images and creating wiki pages until the workspace is trusted.

virtualWorkspaces.description: Editing works; features that depend on the local filesystem (image upload, wiki page creation, asset resolution) are unavailable in virtual workspaces.

commands.title: Open with vMarkd to the side

editor/title.when: !isInDiffEditor && activeCustomEditorId != vmarkd.editor && (resourceExtname == .md || resourceExtname == .markdown) && (resourceScheme == file || resourceScheme == untitled)
!isInDiffEditor && activeCustomEditorId != vmarkd.editor && (resourceExtname == .md || resourceExtname == .markdown) && (resourceScheme == file || resourceScheme == untitled)

enumDescriptions: Match the VS Code theme's colors, following the active light/dark theme.
Render the markdown like GitHub's light theme, regardless of the VS Code theme.
Render the markdown like GitHub's dark theme, regardless of the VS Code theme.
Material Dark (One Dark) palette, regardless of the VS Code theme.
VS Code's built-in markdown preview — light, regardless of the VS Code theme.
VS Code's built-in markdown preview — dark, regardless of the VS Code theme.
vmarkd.theme.content.description: How the rendered markdown looks. "auto" follows the VS Code theme's colors (light/dark); the named themes render with their own palette regardless of the VS Code theme. Code-block syntax colors are set separately by "Syntax Highlight Theme".

enumDescriptions: Follow the VS Code light/dark theme — github in a light theme, github-dark in a dark one.
vmarkd.theme.code.description: Syntax-highlight theme for code blocks. "auto" follows the VS Code theme; otherwise choose a specific theme.

enumDescriptions: Follow the VS Code editor theme — uses the content theme's paired palette if any (e.g. github-light/dark), else mermaid's own dark/default (a plain flowchart looks mostly monochrome).
Force the dark palette regardless of the editor theme.
GitHub — light (matches the github-light content theme).
GitHub — dark (matches the github-dark content theme).
vmarkd.theme.mermaid.description: Color theme for Mermaid diagrams. "auto" follows the VS Code theme; otherwise choose a specific theme.

enumDescriptions: Follow the rendering theme (vmarkd.theme.content) — pairs each content theme with a matching palette (e.g. github-light/dark); when the content theme is "auto", follows the VS Code editor's own colors.
Vintage — muted, warm retro palette (light background).
Vintage Dark — the vintage palette on a warm dark background.
vmarkd.theme.echarts.description: Color theme for ECharts charts. "auto" follows the rendering theme (and the VS Code editor when that is "auto"); otherwise choose a neutral light/dark or one of ECharts' built-in gallery themes.

vmarkd.theme.highlightHeadings.description: Apply a themed background to headings (h1–h6) for easier scanning.

vmarkd.editor.fullWidth.description: Enable full-width layout for the editor (removes max-width centering).

vmarkd.editor.fontSize.description: Base font size of the editor content. "editor" follows VS Code's editor font size (recommended), "vditor" keeps Vditor's larger 16px reading size, or enter a number for an explicit pixel size (e.g. "15"). Headings scale proportionally.

vmarkd.editor.headingMarkers.description: Show the IR-mode left-gutter markers (heading levels H1–H6, the link-reference ↩, etc.). Turn off to hide them and tighten the left margin.

vmarkd.editor.codeLineNumbers.description: Show line numbers in fenced code blocks (rendered preview). Off by default — adds a line-number gutter per code block.

vmarkd.editor.toolbar.description: Show the editor toolbar. When off, the editor opens without a toolbar.

vmarkd.editor.linkOpenWithModifier.description: Follow links with Ctrl+click (Cmd+click on macOS); a plain click places the caret in the link for editing. Turn off to open links on a plain click instead.

vmarkd.css.external.description: Paths to external CSS files (absolute, or relative to the workspace root) injected into the editor. They reload live when edited. customCss is loaded after them, so it always wins on conflicting rules. Resource-scoped — a workspace/folder can override it in `.vscode/settings.json`.

vmarkd.outline.openByDefault.description: Open the outline panel automatically when an editor opens.

vmarkd.outline.treeView.description: Show the 'Markdown Outline' tree in the Explorer sidebar (a clickable heading list for the open markdown file). Turn off to hide it.

vmarkd.outline.position.description: Which side the outline panel opens on.

vmarkd.outline.highlight.description: Briefly flash a heading when you click it in the outline.

vmarkd.image.saveFolder.description: Folder for uploaded images. Default: next to the markdown file; use `${projectRoot}/assets` for the project root. Resource-scoped — a workspace/folder can override it in `.vscode/settings.json`.

enumDescriptions: Keep uploaded/pasted images in their original format and bytes.
Convert raster uploads to WebP (smaller files). SVG and animated GIF are always kept as-is.
vmarkd.image.format.description: Format for uploaded/pasted images. `webp` re-encodes rasters (PNG/JPEG/…) to WebP in the editor before saving — smaller files, link is rewritten to the `.webp` name. SVG (vector) and GIF (animation) are never converted. Falls back to the original bytes if encoding fails.

vmarkd.image.quality.markdownDescription: WebP quality (1–100) when `#vmarkd.image.format#` is `webp`. Higher is larger and sharper; 80 is a good size/quality balance. Ignored for `original`.

vmarkd.image.maxWidth.markdownDescription: Downscale uploaded/pasted images wider than this many pixels (aspect ratio preserved; never upscaled). `0` disables scaling. Applies to both `webp` and `original` (re-encoded when scaled). Set e.g. `1600` to cap large screenshots.

vmarkd.image.allowRemoteImages.markdownDescription: Allow images from remote `https:` URLs (e.g. `![](https://…)`) to load in the editor. **Off by default**: a remote image — or an inline `style="background:url(https://…)"` — is a tracking/exfiltration channel (the request leaks that you opened the file, plus your IP). Local images (`asWebviewUri`), `data:` and `blob:` always work. Turn on only for trusted documents. Resource-scoped — a workspace/folder can override it in `.vscode/settings.json`. Takes effect when the editor is reopened (the security policy is fixed at load).

vmarkd.wiki.enabled.description: Enable wiki-link rendering ([[Page Name]] chips, navigation, and page creation).

vmarkd.wiki.root.description: Wiki root folder (relative to the workspace root). Leave empty to use the workspace root. Example: `docs/wiki`.

vmarkd.advanced.instantPreview.description: Instant preview: before the live editor finishes loading, paint a fast read-only preview of the document and a placeholder toolbar so the file appears immediately, then swap to the live editor seamlessly. Turn off to open straight into the live editor with no pre-render.

vmarkd.advanced.streamLargeFiles.description: Stream very large markdown files into the editor in chunks instead of one blocking render (which can freeze the editor for seconds on big files). Activates automatically for files of about 700 KB or larger. While streaming, the editor is briefly read-only with a loading spinner. Turn off to render large files in a single pass.

vmarkd.advanced.retainHidden.description: Keep hidden editor tabs alive for instant switching. Turn off to free memory when many tabs are open (each hidden editor is fully retained), at the cost of a reload when you switch back. Changing this requires reloading the window.

vmarkd.advanced.contentVisibility.description: Speed up large documents by letting the browser skip layout/paint of off-screen blocks (CSS content-visibility). Activates automatically for files of about 100 KB or larger (no upper limit). Prevents the whole window from freezing on tab switch with big files. Turn off if you hit rendering glitches. Applies when you reopen the file.
